description: Public opinion in Hong Kong regarding the government's handling of the SARS epidemic in early 2003, specifically focusing on the performance of the Hospital Authority and the Secretary for Health, Yeoh Eng-kiong. The crisis led to massive protests in July 2003 and a significant loss of trust in the administration's ability to manage public health emergencies.

key_events.description: Prince of Wales Hospital outbreak begins identification.
Amoy Gardens cluster reported, sparking public panic.
Legislative Council hearings on government response failures.
Mass protests on July 1st largely driven by governance and health handling dissatisfaction.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling public trust decay during a rapid-onset crisis and the impact of perceived institutional incompetence.
